title: UTC+5:30

infobox.key: [Lists of time zones](/wiki/Lists_of_time_zones "Lists of time zones")
infobox.value: * [Time zones by country](/wiki/List_of_time_zones_by_country "List of time zones by country") * [Time zones by UTC offset](/wiki/List_of_UTC_offsets "List of UTC offsets") * [Military time zone](/wiki/Military_time_zone "Military time zone") * [Time zone abbreviations](/wiki/List_of_time_zone_abbreviations "List of time zone abbreviations") * [Daylight saving time by country](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_by_country "Daylight saving time by country")
* [Abolition of time zones](/wiki/Abolition_of_time_zones "Abolition of time zones")

is an identifier for a [time offset](/wiki/UTC_offset "UTC offset") of 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Indian Standard Time](/wiki/Indian_Standard_Time "Indian Standard Time"), used in India, and the [Sri Lanka Standard Time](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_Standard_Time "Sri Lanka Standard Time"), followed in Sri Lanka, are based on the same.[[2]](#cite_note-2) and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It has the second-largest population among time zones, after [UTC+08:00](/wiki/UTC%2B08:00 "UTC+08:00").[[4]](#cite_note-4)
Kornberg, Sara. ["Understanding UTC Offsets"](https://www.timeanddate.com/time/utc-offsets.html). *Time and Date*. Retrieved 1 June 2025.
["Which Time Zone Has the Biggest Population?"](https://www.worldtimeserver.com/learn/which-time-zone-has-the-biggest-population/). *World Time Server*. Retrieved 1 June 2025.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [UTC+5:30](/w/index.php?title=UTC%2B5:30&redirect=no "UTC+5:30"))

Time zone

| UTC+05:30 | |
| --- | --- |
| [Time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") | |
| World map with the time zone highlighted | |
| UTC offset | |
| UTC | UTC+05:30 |
| Current time | |
| 10:35, 26 March 2026 UTC+05:30 [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=UTC%2B05:30&action=purge)] | |
| Central [meridian](/wiki/Meridian_(geography) "Meridian (geography)") | |
| 82.5 degrees E | |
| [Date-time group](/wiki/Date-time_group "Date-time group") | |
| E\* | |

**UTC+5:30** is an identifier for a [time offset](/wiki/UTC_offset "UTC offset") of 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Indian Standard Time](/wiki/Indian_Standard_Time "Indian Standard Time"), used in India, and the [Sri Lanka Standard Time](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_Standard_Time "Sri Lanka Standard Time"), followed in Sri Lanka, are based on the same.[[2]](#cite_note-2) and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It has the second-largest population among time zones, after [UTC+08:00](/wiki/UTC%2B08:00 "UTC+08:00").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The [Nepal Standard Time](/wiki/Nepal_Standard_Time "Nepal Standard Time"), followed by Nepal, used the time zone till 1986, when it switched to [UTC+5:45](/wiki/UTC%2B5:45 "UTC+5:45").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
